headline: UK to regulate cloud service providers Microsoft, Google and others to protect financial stability
summary: Britain has designated cloud service providers Microsoft, Google, Amazon and Oracle as ‌critical third-party suppliers to its financial sector, bringing them ‌under direct regulatory oversight. The move is aimed at strengthening the resilience ​of financial firms by reducing the risk of widespread disruption from cyber attacks or technology outages. "As banks, insurers and financial market infrastructures become increasingly reliant on cloud services, disruption ‌at a major ⁠supplier could affect multiple firms at the same time, potentially impacting services customers depend on," ⁠the government said in a statement on Friday.

raw.headline: UK to regulate cloud service providers Microsoft, Google and others to protect financial stability
raw.summary: Britain has designated cloud service providers Microsoft, Google, Amazon and Oracle as ‌critical third-party suppliers to its financial sector, bringing them ‌under direct regulatory oversight. The move is aimed at strengthening the resilience ​of financial firms by reducing the risk of widespread disruption from cyber attacks or technology outages. "As banks, insurers and financial market infrastructures become increasingly reliant on cloud services, disruption ‌at a major ⁠supplier could affect multiple firms at the same time, potentially impacting services customers depend on," ⁠the government said in a statement on Friday.

headline: This French Water Company Doesn’t Make Bottled Water. It’s Bigger Than That.
summary: Name a French water company besides Perrier or Evian.  How about  Veolia Environnement  a large global environmental services company that produces and distributes drinking water and treats wastewater (and has waste management and energy businesses).  The company, then called Compagnie Générale des Eaux, or CGE, was founded in 1853 by Napoleon III’s imperial decree to supply France with drinking water.
